theory.intro: In 24-hour time the day runs from $0000$ to $2359$, so the hours keep counting past noon instead of starting again at $1$.
facts: 24-hour time uses **four digits**: the first two are the hour, the last two the minutes (e.g. $1430$).
From $1300$ onwards the hour is more than $12$ — these are afternoon and evening times.
steps: Read the first two digits as the hour ($00$–$23$).
Read the last two digits as the minutes ($00$–$59$).
Hours of $13$ or more are in the afternoon or evening.
